meta.title: CJNG Cartel Retaliatory Violence — Nationwide, Feb 22–25, 2026
meta.subtitle: 252+ narcobloqueos across 20+ states following the killing of "El Mencho" in Tapalpa, Jalisco

infoBox.vehicleTypes: Trucks, buses, taxis, cars, Oxxo stores, banks, government buildings
infoBox.status: Phase 1 (Feb 22-23): 90% cleared by Sunday night. Phase 2 (Feb 24+): Continued in Jalisco.
infoBox.coordNote: 74+ dead (27 security forces, 46+ CJNG/criminals, civilians). 70+ arrested. 10,000 soldiers deployed.

incidents.title: Bus burned at Las Cruces distribuidor vial, Acapulco
incidents.detail: Armed civilians intercepted public transit bus on ruta Hospital–Vacacional–Sector 6 Centro at Distribuidor vial de Las Cruces (zona suburbana), forced all passengers and driver off at gunpoint, doused bus with gasoline and set it on fire. Vehicle: camión de transporte público. Route heading toward carretera federal Acapulco–México. First emergency call of day; fire department and Guardia Nacional responded.

incidents.title: Camioneta burned at Bulevar Vicente Guerrero, Coppel store, Ciudad Renacimiento
incidents.detail: Armed men stopped camioneta de pasajeros directly in front of Coppel department store on Bulevar Vicente Guerrero, Colonia Ciudad Renacimiento. Forced driver and passengers out at gunpoint, doused van with gasoline, set ablaze on lateral lanes. Boulevard effectively shut down. Military snipers took position on pedestrian bridge spanning boulevard connecting Colonia Zapata to Ciudad Renacimiento. All department stores, tortillerías, and shops in surrounding area closed. Witness account: 'terror scene'; ruta Vacacional–Cima–Centro bus burning approximately six blocks away.

incidents.title: Camioneta tipo Urvan burned on Calzada Pie de la Cuesta, route to Zihuatanejo
incidents.detail: Armed men burned camioneta tipo Urvan operating ruta Pie de la Cuesta–Centro on Calzada Pie de la Cuesta (main road from Acapulco northwest toward Costa Grande highway toward Zihuatanejo). Vehicle completely calcined. Passengers exited before fire. Gateway from Acapulco toward Coyuca de Benítez, Atoyac, Petatlán, and Zihuatanejo/Ixtapa. Main exit route northwest from Acapulco effectively blocked.

incidents.title: Tráiler burned at Caseta La Venta toll plaza, Autopista del Sol
incidents.detail: Armed individuals positioned tráiler (semi-truck) across highway at Caseta de peaje La Venta on carretera federal Acapulco–México (Autopista del Sol), km 366+100 heading toward Chilpancingo. Set tractor-trailer on fire, completely blocking main highway to Chilpancingo, Iguala, Cuernavaca, Mexico City. CAPUFE (federal highway authority) officially reported closure of La Venta toll plaza and partial closure of Autopista del Sol at km 366. Circulation restored later in day. Stranded numerous travelers heading to/from Acapulco.

incidents.title: Attack on PIM headquarters, Colonia Emiliano Zapata, Acapulco
incidents.detail: Multiple armed individuals riding motorcycles opened fire on facade of Comandancia de la Policía Investigadora Ministerial (PIM), state attorney general's (FGE) investigative police building at Colonia Emiliano Zapata. Two wounded: one Guardia Nacional officer and one civilian, both out of danger. Building suffered significant bullet damage to exterior. FGE opened formal investigation (carpeta de investigación).

incidents.title: Complete transit and commercial shutdown across Acapulco
incidents.detail: Acabús (bus rapid transit) suspended all service 5 hours. All urban bus routes suspended. Camionetas alimentadoras suspended. Estrella de Oro and Costa Line long-distance buses suspended service 9 hours. Bus terminals in Chilpancingo canceled departures to Acapulco. Centro comercial La Gran Plaza, shops along Costera and suburbs closed. Archbishop Leopoldo González ordered all churches in port closed. All public and private schools announced suspension for Monday Feb 23.

incidents.title: Acapulco International Airport flight delays and Abierto Mexicano de Tenis security
incidents.detail: Aeropuerto Internacional de Acapulco General Juan N. Álvarez flights delayed ~1 hour due to surrounding violence and road closures affecting airport access. Airport itself not directly attacked. Arena GNP Seguros tournament (Abierto Mexicano de Tenis) continued as scheduled despite violence; organizers denied cancellation rumors. Governor Salgado deployed special security plan with 750+ operatives.

incidents.title: Cargo truck burned at Libramiento a Pantla, La Salitrera, Zihuatanejo
incidents.detail: Armed men forced driver of cargo truck carrying packages from Estado de México to exit at gunpoint at Libramiento a Pantla, La Salitrera (northwest outskirts on bypass road). Truck doused with gasoline and set on fire. SEMAR (Mexican Navy), Guardia Nacional, and firefighters responded. First reported Zihuatanejo incident.

incidents.title: Compact cars burned in Colonia Nuevo Amanecer, Zihuatanejo
incidents.detail: Armed men with gasoline set fire to parked automobile in Colonia Nuevo Amanecer alongside Bulevar Paseo de Zihuatanejo. Additional compact vehicles burned in area shortly after.

incidents.title: Vehicles burned in Colonia La Noria, Zihuatanejo
incidents.detail: Compact vehicles set on fire in Colonia La Noria alongside Bulevar Paseo de Zihuatanejo. Later incident (~6:00 PM) saw another automobile burned near La Noria market; neighbors helped control fire until firefighters arrived.

incidents.title: Las Dos Costas Urvans and private car burned on Carretera Zihuatanejo–Lázaro Cárdenas
incidents.detail: Armed men burned two Urvan-type public transit vans of 'Las Dos Costas' transport company plus one private automobile on Carretera federal Zihuatanejo–Lázaro Cárdenas near Lagunillas and San Jerónimo (southeast toward La Unión/Michoacán border). Urvans served routes to Petatlán and La Unión. Armed men entered Las Dos Costas company headquarters, forcibly removed vehicles, and burned them. Crew: passengers forced out at gunpoint.

incidents.title: Attempted Urvan burning at Chedraui supermarket, airport route, Zihuatanejo
incidents.detail: Armed men attempted to burn public transit Urvan on airport route in front of Chedraui supermarket near Colonia Nuevo Amanecer. Firefighters transiting area responded quickly and prevented vehicle from being fully destroyed. Partial burn prevented.

incidents.title: Car burned on Calle Palmas, downtown Zihuatanejo
incidents.detail: Compact automobile set on fire on Calle Palmas in centro of Zihuatanejo. Multi-agency response: firefighters, SEMAR (Navy), Ejército Mexicano (Army), Policía Estatal, Guardia Nacional, Policía Municipal. Downtown location symbolized CJNG control.

incidents.title: Vehicle burnings in outlying Zihuatanejo communities
incidents.detail: Vehicle burnings reported in El Embalse, Coacoyul, Agua de Correa, and Playa Las Salinas communities scattered around Zihuatanejo municipality. In Agua de Correa, armed men set fire to vehicle with gasoline. At least one motorcycle burned during period. Limited details suggest coordination across multiple outlying areas.

incidents.title: Commerce and tourism shutdown in Zihuatanejo and Ixtapa
incidents.detail: Sam's Club, Soriana, Walmart, Oxxo all closed preemptively. All public transit suspended from 3:00 PM. Transit to Petatlán, La Unión, Lázaro Cárdenas suspended. Primera Plus and Estrella Blanca halted service. Resorts sheltered guests (86.8% occupancy Ixtapa, 76.7% Zihuatanejo). American traveler Hilary Hutler turned back by 20 police cars, saw smoke, diverted to Ixtapa hotel nearly full with sheltering travelers. By 6:20 PM all roads reopened. 32 flights canceled at Guadalajara airport. Schools suspended Monday Feb 23.

incidents.title: Military Operation Kills El Mencho in Tapalpa
incidents.detail: Federal forces killed Nemesio Oseguera Cervantes ('El Mencho'), CJNG leader, in mountainous municipality of Tapalpa (southern Jalisco). Rural compound provided concealment. Operation began 7:15 AM with units in assault positions. Ground and helicopter-borne special forces engaged 7:20 AM-10:30 AM (3-hour firefight). One helicopter struck by cartel rocket fire (9:45 AM) but no military casualties. El Mencho airlifted 10:15 AM, pronounced dead at hospital. All 8 escorts/security killed at scene. Military: Mexican Army Special Forces, National Guard (200-300 personnel), 6 helicopters (Bell 407, UH-60 Black Hawk), 2-3 Texan (T-6) aircraft, medical evacuation teams. CIA/JITC-CC intelligence support provided signal, human, and imagery intelligence.

incidents.title: Death of El Tuli (Hugo César Macías Ureña), CJNG operational lieutenant
incidents.detail: Hugo César Macías Ureña ('El Tuli'), CJNG principal operational lieutenant serving under El Mencho, maintained command center in El Grullo (60 km from Tapalpa). Upon El Mencho's death, immediately activated pre-planned national retaliation protocol. Military identified his location while coordinating blockade operations on road near El Grullo. Pursued and killed in firefight before reaching safety. Removal of retaliation coordinator did not stop cascade—indicates pre-planned, distributed campaign to regional commanders.

incidents.title: Guadalajara metropolitan blockade campaign - 37 locations
incidents.detail: Coordinated blockade at 37 documented locations across Guadalajara metro (6 municipalities). Gas stations on fire, multiple banks attacked. Public transit suspended: Mi Macro Bus, Tren Eléctrico. CJNG distributed threat videos and circulars demanding government negotiations. Major blockades: Periférico (tanker truck/fuel truck fire blocking north-south routes, 6+ hours, 15+ km backup), Avenida México, Calle Federalismo. Buenavista District Carretera Guadalajara–Morelia (km 40, 4 hrs). Avenida Adolfo B. Horn/Ventorro (3.5 hrs). Camino a San Isidro Mazatepec/Pino Suárez (4 hrs). Camino a la Soledad/Allende (3 hrs). Avenida Independencia/Juárez (burned vehicle, 5 hrs). Tanker truck created hazmat incident; toxic fumes forced emergency protocols; Mexican hazmat teams required. Strategic blockade sealed primary route to Saltillo and northern Mexico.

incidents.title: Zapopan blockade campaign - 12+ locations
incidents.detail: Zapopan (1.4 million residents, second-largest in metro) experienced systematic blockades paralyzing municipality Feb 22-23. Periférico/Carretera a Saltillo (tanker fire, north-south arterial 6+ hrs, 15+ km backup, hazmat incident). Bosques de San Isidro/Carretera a Saltillo (secondary, 5 hrs). Periférico/Vallarta (western loop, 4 hrs). Carretera a Nogales/Allende (north border routes, 4.5 hrs). Venta del Astillero and additional locations. 8 Oxxo stores burned on Avenida Mariano Otero. 2 commercial banks torched. 3 police vehicles damaged, 2 officers wounded. Heavy armed gunmen presence near Plaza Patria.

incidents.title: Puerto Vallarta blockades and fires on Highway 200 and downtown
incidents.detail: 15+ narcobloqueos on Avenida Francisco Medina Ascencio and Highway 200. Multiple hotel zones affected, tourism paralyzed. 6 buses burned on roadways. Police presence minimal. CJNG maintained blockades 36+ hours with rotating armed personnel. Coordinated attacks across tourism infrastructure including hotel access roads.

incidents.title: Puerto Vallarta Penitentiary prison break
incidents.detail: Armed CJNG coordinated direct assault on Penitenciaría de Puerto Vallarta, 11:00 AM-12:30 PM. Heavy vehicle driven at main gate, destroyed entrance gate. Armed men with assault weapons opened fire on guards, moved to holding cells, released pre-identified prisoners. Casualties: 1 Penitentiary Police Officer killed in firefight, several guards wounded. 23 prisoners escaped (mid-level CJNG operatives and associates awaiting trial). Attack demonstrated ability to locate imprisoned members, plan extractions, execute during national chaos. National Guard and state police arrived after assault team and escaped prisoners departed.

incidents.title: Guadalajara International Airport disruption
incidents.detail: 49 flights canceled (25%), 42 delayed (22%). Carriers affected: American, Delta, United, Southwest, Alaska, WestJet, Air Canada. Panic in terminals. Reports of external gunfire around airport perimeter. Major disruption to international and domestic travel.

incidents.title: Ciudad Guzmán blockades and Oxxo fires
incidents.detail: Partial blockades on Avenida Jacarandas and bypass routes. 3 Oxxo stores looted and burned. 1 bank ATM destroyed with explosives. 8 vehicles abandoned on streets. Highways to Colima partially affected. National Guard deployed by afternoon.

incidents.title: Chapala highway blockade and shooting
incidents.detail: Narcobloqueos and active shootings on highway from Guadalajara to Chapala.

incidents.title: Tecolotlán road blockades and vehicle burnings
incidents.detail: Road blockades and vehicle burnings throughout Tecolotlán municipality.

incidents.title: Mascota highway blockade toward Puerto Vallarta
incidents.detail: Road blockade on mountain highway toward Puerto Vallarta.

incidents.title: Tlajomulco commercial attacks and vehicle burnings
incidents.detail: 10+ commercial establishments attacked on Avenida Las Fuentes. 5 Oxxo stores burned, 2 pharmacies looted, 1 mini-supermarket destroyed. Cash registers emptied before fires set. 4 vehicles burned on nearby streets. Coordinated attack on commercial targets.

incidents.title: Car bomb attack in San Juan de los Lagos
incidents.detail: Car bomb detonated in town center on Avenida Benito Juárez. Destroyed 2 nearby vehicles, damaged storefront. 1 civilian killed, 4 wounded. 50-meter blast radius. Attack claimed to target police informants.

incidents.title: León mass arson campaign - 20+ stores
incidents.detail: León city: 20+ stores attacked on Avenida Universidad and Paseo de la República. 8 Oxxo stores burned, 4 gas stations vandalized. 2 vehicles exploded near El Bajío neighborhood. Coordinated teams operating from 22:00 to 04:00 hours.

incidents.title: Guanajuato city arson attacks on commercial establishments
incidents.detail: Incendiary attacks on multiple commercial establishments in Guanajuato capital.

incidents.title: Celaya arson campaign on Oxxo and commercial targets
incidents.detail: Multiple Oxxo stores and commercial establishments burned in Celaya.

incidents.title: Salamanca arson attacks on commercial establishments
incidents.detail: Coordinated arson campaign on commercial establishments in Salamanca.

incidents.title: Irapuato Oxxo store arson
incidents.detail: Multiple Oxxo stores and businesses burned in Irapuato.

incidents.title: Lázaro Cárdenas port and highway blockade
incidents.detail: Major blockade on carretera federal at Lázaro Cárdenas (major port city). Port operations disrupted. Federal highway access sealed. Multiple vehicles burned. Strategic port city connecting to Zihuatanejo and Guerrero.

incidents.title: La Ruana area blockades and armed confrontations
incidents.detail: Heavy violence in La Ruana region with blockades on highway. Multiple armed confrontations between CJNG and security forces. Heavy armed presence.

incidents.title: Buenavista–Tepalcatepec corridor violence
incidents.detail: Heavy CJNG violence in Buenavista–Tepalcatepec corridor bordering Jalisco. Multiple blockades and vehicle burnings. Disputed territory between cartels.

incidents.title: Apatzingán highway blockade
incidents.detail: Road blockade on highway near Apatzingán with vehicle burnings.

incidents.title: Huetamo area blockades near Guerrero border
incidents.detail: Blockades in Huetamo area bordering Guerrero. CJNG cell 'El Suavecito' operates in adjacent Guerrero Tierra Caliente.

incidents.title: Playa Azul port area disruption
incidents.detail: Port area disrupted with commercial closures. Port security response.

incidents.title: Arteaga road blockade
incidents.detail: Road blockade in Arteaga municipality.

incidents.title: Manzanillo port blockade
incidents.detail: Major blockade at Manzanillo port (critical Pacific port for CJNG). Multiple vehicles burned on access roads. Port operations disrupted.

incidents.title: Colima city blockades and fires
incidents.detail: Multiple blockades on outskirts of Colima capital. Vehicle burnings. Commercial closures.

incidents.title: Tecomán blockade
incidents.detail: Road blockade near Tecomán.

incidents.title: Bahía de Banderas highway blockade
incidents.detail: Major blockade on carretera federal along Bahía de Banderas coast. Multiple vehicles burned. Tourism and commercial traffic disrupted.

incidents.title: Nuevo Vallarta area blockades
incidents.detail: Blockades around Nuevo Vallarta resort area. Tourism infrastructure affected.

incidents.title: Sayulita tourism area blockade
incidents.detail: Blockade in Sayulita tourist area with vehicle burnings.

incidents.title: Cancún highway blockade and commercial disruption
incidents.detail: Major blockade on carretera federal near Cancún. Tourism paralyzed. Multiple hotels reported lockdowns. Highway 307 partially closed.

incidents.title: Playa del Carmen hotel zone disruption
incidents.detail: Blockades affecting Playa del Carmen hotel zone. Tourists sheltered in place. Highway 307 access disrupted.

incidents.title: Tulum highway blockade
incidents.detail: Road blockade near Tulum affecting Highway 307.

incidents.title: Laredo border crossing blockade
incidents.detail: Major blockade on highway approaching Laredo port of entry. Cross-border trade disrupted. Multiple vehicles burned on access roads.

incidents.title: Matamoros and Nuevo Laredo corridor blockade
incidents.detail: Multiple blockades on carretera federal between Matamoros and Nuevo Laredo. Commercial traffic halted.

incidents.title: Reynosa highway blockade
incidents.detail: Road blockade near Reynosa affecting access to border crossing.

incidents.title: Puebla city central highway blockade
incidents.detail: Major blockade on central highway through Puebla affecting access to Mexico City corridor.

incidents.title: Puebla Oxxo stores arson campaign
incidents.detail: Multiple Oxxo stores burned in Puebla city.

incidents.title: Tehuacán highway blockade
incidents.detail: Road blockade on highway near Tehuacán.

incidents.title: Atotonilco el Alto ambush - National Guard losses
incidents.detail: Coordinated ambush of National Guard patrol in Atotonilco el Alto. 8 National Guard soldiers killed in engagement with CJNG. Casualties highlight military losses during retaliation period.

incidents.title: Teocaltiche military base attack
incidents.detail: Attack on military installations at Teocaltiche base. 1 soldier killed in firefight.

incidents.title: La Barca area highway patrol interdiction
incidents.detail: Highway patrol interdiction in La Barca area with armed clashes.

incidents.title: Las Cañadas remote area engagement
incidents.detail: Remote region engagement between CJNG and military forces in Las Cañadas area.

incidents.title: Lagos de Moreno secondary engagement
incidents.detail: Secondary engagement during blockade operations in Lagos de Moreno area.

incidents.title: Intersection of Avenida de la Mancha and Calle Covadonga, Zapopan
incidents.detail: CJNG members forcibly stopped vehicles to burn them at intersection of Avenida de la Mancha and Calle Covadonga, Zapopan. National Guard units engaged cartel members. 1 civilian woman (3 months pregnant) killed in crossfire. Death highlighted indiscriminate violence and civilian vulnerability in urban warfare zones.

incidents.title: Jocotepec highway blockade
incidents.detail: Road blockade on highway near Jocotepec.

incidents.title: Ameca highway blockade
incidents.detail: Road blockade on highway near Ameca.

incidents.title: Atemajac de Brizuela blockade
incidents.detail: Road blockade in Atemajac de Brizuela area.

incidents.title: Talpa de Allende highway blockade
incidents.detail: Road blockade on highway toward Talpa de Allende.

incidents.title: San Andrés Ibarra blockade
incidents.detail: Road blockade in San Andrés Ibarra area.

incidents.title: Cihuatlán coastal highway blockade
incidents.detail: Road blockade on coastal highway near Cihuatlán.

incidents.title: Melaque beach area blockade
incidents.detail: Road blockade affecting Melaque beach tourist area.

incidents.title: Coahuayana port area blockade
incidents.detail: Blockade in Coahuayana port area on Pacific coast.

incidents.title: Tancítaro blockade
incidents.detail: Road blockade in Tancítaro area.

incidents.title: Ario de Rosales blockade
incidents.detail: Road blockade in Ario de Rosales municipality.

incidents.title: Tijuana border crossing blockade
incidents.detail: Major blockade on approaches to Tijuana port of entry. Cross-border trade disrupted. Multiple vehicles burned.

incidents.title: Rosarito Beach highway blockade
incidents.detail: Road blockade on highway near Rosarito Beach resort area.

incidents.title: Ensenada harbor area blockade
incidents.detail: Blockade affecting Ensenada port access.

incidents.title: Culiacán highway blockade
incidents.detail: Road blockade on highway near Culiacán.

incidents.title: Durango city highway blockade
incidents.detail: Road blockade on highway near Durango capital.

incidents.title: Zacatecas capital highway blockade
incidents.detail: Road blockade on highway near Zacatecas capital.

incidents.title: Xalapa area highway blockade
incidents.detail: Road blockade on highway near Xalapa.

incidents.title: Periférico and Tabachines - Mi Macro BRT Suspension Point
incidents.detail: Blockade with vehicle burning at Periférico and Tabachines intersection. Mi Macro Periférico Bus Rapid Transit system suspended service between Barranca de Huentitán and Chapalita Inn. BRT remained closed through February 24. ~200,000 daily passengers stranded.

incidents.title: Fatal Crossfire at Av de la Mancha and Covadonga, Zapopan
incidents.detail: National Guard engaged CJNG members systematically forcing drivers to abandon vehicles for arson at Avenida de la Mancha and Calle Covadonga. One woman, 3 months pregnant, killed in crossfire. Her death symbolized indiscriminate nature of cartel retaliation.

incidents.title: SAT Tax Authority Offices Burned, Zapopan
incidents.detail: Multiple Servicio de Administración Tributaria (SAT) tax authority offices burned in Zapopan. Federal tax administration temporarily suspended. Attacks signaled cartel targeting of state revenue collection infrastructure.

incidents.title: Lázaro Cárdenas and Batalla de Zacatecas, Tlaquepaque
incidents.detail: Vehicles burned at Lázaro Cárdenas and Batalla de Zacatecas intersection in Tlaquepaque. Major commercial district access disrupted for 4 hours.

incidents.title: Lázaro Cárdenas and Río Seco, Tlaquepaque
incidents.detail: Vehicles burned at Lázaro Cárdenas and Río Seco intersection. Secondary commercial corridor blocked 3.5 hours.

incidents.title: 69 Oxxo Stores Set on Fire Across Jalisco
incidents.detail: Coordinated arson campaign: 69 Oxxo convenience stores set on fire, additional 81 locations damaged/looted. Armed CJNG members systematically entered stores with incendiary devices. Some stores looted before arson. Concentrated targeting suggested centralized coordination by regional commanders.

incidents.title: 22 Banco del Bienestar Branches Attacked
incidents.detail: 18 Banco del Bienestar government welfare bank branches set on fire, 22 total affected. Attacks on government financial institutions represented symbolic attacks on state authority and social services infrastructure.

incidents.title: Complete Guadalajara Transit System Collapse
incidents.detail: Mi Macro Periférico BRT: complete shutdown from Barranca de Huentitán to Chapalita Inn (~45km). Tren Eléctrico Urbano light rail: multiple stations closed Feb 22-24. All city bus routes suspended Feb 22-24. ~1.5 million residents without public transport. Bus operators threatened; routes passed through blockade zones.

incidents.title: Carretera a La Cumbre, toward Pihuamo
incidents.detail: Road blockade with 2 vehicles burned on Carretera a La Cumbre rumbo a Pihuamo. Cut off eastern corridor. Duration 3.5 hours.

incidents.title: Municipality of Comala Blockade
incidents.detail: Blockade in Comala municipality. Major agricultural municipality isolated. Duration 3 hours.

incidents.title: Zona Costera de Armería Blockade
incidents.detail: Blockade in coastal zone of Armería. Coastal communities cut off from main state highway. Duration 4 hours.

incidents.title: Tecomán-Cerro de Ortega Highway
incidents.detail: Highway blockade on Tecomán-Cerro de Ortega road. Sealed route in southern region. 3.5 hours.

incidents.title: Manzanillo-Minatitlán Road Blockade
incidents.detail: Blockade on Manzanillo-Minatitlán road. Port access road blocked; maritime operations disrupted. 3+ hours.

incidents.title: Carretera Colima-Tepames Blockade
incidents.detail: Road blockade with 1 vehicle burned on Carretera Colima-Tepames. Regional route sealed. 3 hours.

incidents.title: Carretera Villa de Álvarez-Minatitlán, 3 vehicles burned
incidents.detail: Road blockade with 3 vehicles burned on Carretera Villa de Álvarez-Minatitlán. Major route to Manzanillo port sealed. 4+ hours.

incidents.title: Guayabo Curve Primary Blockade, Tepic-Xalisco
incidents.detail: 4+ vehicles completely burned at Guayabo curve on Tepic-Xalisco Boulevard: 2 automobiles + 2 trucks. All positioned to block highway. Intense fires visible across capital city. Tepic-Xalisco corridor sealed 5+ hours.

incidents.title: Lateral Lane to Puerto Vallarta Blockade
incidents.detail: 2 automobiles + 2 trucks burned on lateral lane toward Puerto Vallarta/Compostela. Alternate route sealed. Cut off southern access from Tepic. 5+ hours.

incidents.title: Technological University of Xalisco Blockade
incidents.detail: Vehicles burned near Technological University of Xalisco campus. University lockdown; students sheltered in place; campus access restricted.

incidents.title: Puente de los Lobos Bridge Blockade, Tepic
incidents.detail: Blockade at Puente de los Lobos (Bridge of the Wolves) in Tepic. 3+ hours duration.

incidents.title: Tepic-Mazatlán Highway Blockade
incidents.detail: Vehicles burned on highway heading toward Mazatlán. North-south corridor to Sinaloa disrupted. 4+ hours.

incidents.title: Federal Highway 15 Blockade after Ejido San Cayetano
incidents.detail: Blockade on Federal Highway 15 after Ejido San Cayetano. Major north-south corridor disrupted. 3.5 hours.

incidents.title: Nayarit Código Rojo Activation
incidents.detail: Nayarit activated Código Rojo (Red Code) — highest state emergency alert. All government agencies mobilized. Schools closed. Commerce suspended. Military deployed. Maintained through Feb 23.

incidents.title: Cancún Free Highway - 3 Vehicles Burned
incidents.detail: 3 vehicles burned on Carretera Libre (free highway) to Cancún. Tourism access routes blocked. Long-haul traffic diverted. 3-4 hours.

incidents.title: Cancún-Leona Vicario Road Blockade
incidents.detail: Blockade/temporary closure on Cancún-Leona Vicario road. Alternative route sealed. 3+ hours.

incidents.title: Playa del Carmen - 7 Vehicles Burned including Centro Maya
incidents.detail: 7 vehicles burned at various locations throughout Playa del Carmen. 2 automobiles burned in parking lot of Centro Maya high-end shopping plaza. Tourism circulation disrupted. International media coverage.

incidents.title: Federal Highway 307 at Aldea Tulum
incidents.detail: 1 vehicle burned at entrance to Aldea Tulum residential development on Federal Highway 307 (major coastal corridor). Road affected 3+ hours.

incidents.title: Tulum-Cobá Road - 2 Vehicles Burned near Macario Gómez
incidents.detail: 2 vehicles burned on Carretera Tulum-Cobá before community of Macario Gómez. Road closed 4 hours. Inland access to Cobá ruins disrupted.

incidents.title: Cozumel Island - Bodega Aurrerá and Oxxo
incidents.detail: Bodega Aurrerá supermarket on Cozumel island deliberately torched. Major retail operation destroyed. Additional Oxxo store damaged. Supply disruptions on island.

incidents.title: Reynosa Libramiento at Colonia Cumbres
incidents.detail: Blockade with vehicle fires on Libramiento (ring road) connecting Reynosa to Monterrey at Colonia Cumbres. Major exit route sealed. 3-4 hours.

incidents.title: Carretera Matamoros-Reynosa at Sector Las Urracas
incidents.detail: Vehicle fires and tire burning (ponchallantas) on Carretera Matamoros-Reynosa at Sector Las Urracas. North-south corridor sealed. 3-4 hours.

incidents.title: Bulevar Miguel Hidalgo Multiple Blockades
incidents.detail: Vehicle blockades and fires at multiple locations on Bulevar Miguel Hidalgo y Costilla — Reynosa's primary commercial corridor. Sub-locations: Oriente Dos, Valle Alto, Vista Hermosa. Restaurants, shops, offices disrupted. 3-5 hours.

incidents.title: Carretera a Río Bravo at Colonia Aeropuerto
incidents.detail: Vehicle fires and blockade on Carretera a Río Bravo at Colonia Aeropuerto. Access to Reynosa airport and Río Bravo municipality disrupted.

incidents.title: Nuevo Laredo Blockades and Fires
incidents.detail: Blockades and vehicles burned throughout Nuevo Laredo. Major border crossing; U.S.-Mexico trade corridor disrupted. Feb 22-23.

incidents.title: Matamoros Strategic Road Closures
incidents.detail: Strategic roads closed with vehicles parked across; tire spikes (ponchallantas) deployed. Major border city isolated. Feb 22-23.

incidents.title: México-Puebla Highway km 71 - Tráiler Burned
incidents.detail: Tráiler (semi-trailer truck) burned at kilometer 71 on México-Puebla autopista near Santa Rita Tlahuapan, paraje Los Cochinitos. Major cargo blockade.

incidents.title: México-Puebla Highway km 76 - Public Transit Truck Burned
incidents.detail: Camión de transporte público (public transportation truck) burned at km 76. Passenger transport disrupted.

incidents.title: México-Puebla Highway km 79 - ADO Bus Burned
incidents.detail: Autobús ADO (major long-distance bus company) burned at km 79. Radio host René Franco narrated real-time the experience of being trapped on an ADO bus near the burning. International media coverage. Total: 8km of autopista sealed by fires at 4 locations.

incidents.title: Puebla-Orizaba Autopista km 264 'Curva de los Marranos'
incidents.detail: Tráiler (semi-trailer) burned at km 264 on Autopista Puebla-Orizaba in zone known as 'Curva de los Marranos'. Highway sealed 4+ hours. Secondary route disrupted when México-Puebla already blocked.

incidents.title: Tihuatlán-Tuxpan Road Blockade, Sierra Norte
incidents.detail: Road blockade in Sierra Norte region on Tihuatlán-Tuxpan road. Remote communities isolated. 3+ hours.

incidents.title: Autopista Tecate-Tijuana Tractor-Trailer Fire at El Hongo
incidents.detail: Tractocamión (tractor-trailer) burned on Autopista Tecate-Tijuana at El Hongo. Total blockade; 10+ km vehicle backup. Hazmat incident from diesel/tire fire. 4+ hours.

incidents.title: Carretera Libre near Tecate - Gunfire
incidents.detail: Gunfire and detonations reported on Carretera Libre (free highway) near Tecate. Panic among motorists; drivers abandoned vehicles and fled on foot. Intermittent over several hours.

incidents.title: Caseta del Hongo Toll Booth Closure
incidents.detail: CAPUFE closed Caseta del Hongo toll booth on carretera Tecate-Mexicali. Armed groups in area. Remained closed through Feb 24. Sealed eastern corridor Tijuana-Mexicali; long-haul trucks forced to desert routes.

incidents.title: Baja California Arrests: 25 Detained Across 29 Incidents
incidents.detail: 25 people detained across 29 incidents statewide. Charges related to blockade participation, vandalism, weapons possession. U.S. Consulate issued shelter-in-place order for all personnel.

incidents.title: Los Caños Highway 71 South - Pickup Truck Fire
incidents.detail: Pickup truck set on fire south of Aguascalientes city at Los Caños community on Highway 71 south. Authorities quickly attended; highway cleared in 2-3 hours. Isolated incident.

incidents.title: Temixco Taxi Fire
incidents.detail: Taxi deliberately burned in Temixco municipality. Local police response.

incidents.title: Jiutepec Store Attacks
incidents.detail: Convenience stores attacked in Jiutepec including Soriana Express on Avenida Las Flores. Adjacent buildings affected by fire spread. 1 person treated for smoke inhalation.

incidents.title: Mexico-Cuernavaca Highway Fire at Tlalpan
incidents.detail: Vehicle fire at Tlalpan on Mexico-Cuernavaca highway. No injuries. Police and firefighters deployed. Central del Norte bus terminal cancelled buses to Jalisco, Guanajuato, Michoacán, Aguascalientes.

incidents.title: Estado de México Federal Highway Blockade
incidents.detail: Narcobloqueos on federal highways in Estado de México. Rapidly attended by authorities.

incidents.title: Escuinapa-Tecualilla Road Blockade
incidents.detail: Road blockades in Tecualilla community of Escuinapa. Attended by authorities; cleared quickly.

incidents.title: Mazatlán Villa Unión Blockades
incidents.detail: Blockades and vehicles vandalized in Villa Unión within Mazatlán municipality. Authorities responded; incidents contained.

incidents.title: Pachuca Area Blockades
incidents.detail: Isolated narcobloqueos in Pachuca region. Immediate local response; quickly resolved.

incidents.title: Monterrey Area Blockades
incidents.detail: Isolated narcobloqueos in Monterrey metropolitan area. Rapid security clearance.

incidents.title: Oaxaca City Blockades
incidents.detail: Isolated narcobloqueos in Oaxaca capital. Local authority response.

incidents.title: Querétaro Area Blockades
incidents.detail: Isolated narcobloqueos in Querétaro region. Authority response.

incidents.title: San Luis Potosí Area Blockades
incidents.detail: Isolated narcobloqueos in San Luis Potosí region.

incidents.title: Veracruz Port Region Blockades
incidents.detail: Narcobloqueos in Veracruz port region. Highway access to port affected.

incidents.title: Orizaba Area Blockades
incidents.detail: Road blockades in Orizaba area affecting Puebla-Veracruz corridor.

incidents.title: Zacatecas City Blockades
incidents.detail: Narcobloqueos in Zacatecas capital.

incidents.title: Tlaxcala City Blockades
incidents.detail: Narcobloqueos in Tlaxcala capital.

incidents.title: Durango Area Blockades
incidents.detail: Isolated blockade incidents in Durango. Local authority response.

incidents.title: Pueblito de Rocha Oxxo Fire near Primary School
incidents.detail: First confirmed Oxxo fire in Guanajuato at Pueblito de Rocha neighborhood. Located meters from Escuela Primaria (primary school). Fire brigade response; minor evacuations from nearby school.

incidents.title: Calle Cantarranas Oxxo near Teatro Principal
incidents.detail: Oxxo burned on Calle Cantarranas adjacent to Teatro Principal (historic theater) in Centro Histórico. Smoke damage to adjacent historic structures. Theater temporarily closed.

incidents.title: Abasolo Entrance Oxxo at Megabandera Glorieta
incidents.detail: Oxxo set on fire at entrance of Abasolo on carretera toward Pénjamo at glorieta de la Megabandera. Town entrance/exit effectively sealed. Duration 3.5 hours.

incidents.title: Purísima del Rincón Oxxo - Woman Burned
incidents.detail: Oxxo arson in Colonia Los Manantiales, Purísima del Rincón. 1 woman injured with burn injuries covering 10% of her body. Victim likely worked at or was shopping at Oxxo when fire started. Transferred to regional burn treatment center.

incidents.title: Moroleón Sabritas Truck Fire
incidents.detail: Sabritas (Mexican snack company) delivery truck burned at entronque del libramiento Sur de Moroleón with carretera toward La Ordeña. Food/snack supply chain disrupted. 4+ hours.

incidents.title: Manuel Doblado Banco del Bienestar Attack
incidents.detail: Banco del Bienestar (government welfare bank) branch attacked in Manuel Doblado. Government banking infrastructure targeted.

incidents.title: León 18 Incidents - Citywide Campaign
incidents.detail: 18 distinct incidents in León (2nd largest city, 1.6M residents). Combination of vehicle fires, blockades, and property damage. Police, fire, and National Guard responding throughout day. Feb 22-23.

incidents.title: Zamora-Jacona Road Blockade
incidents.detail: Vehicle blockade on Zamora-Jacona road. Alternative route between towns blocked. 4 hours.

incidents.title: Zamora-Tangancícuaro Road Blockade
incidents.detail: Vehicle blockade on Zamora-Tangancícuaro road. Third route through region sealed. Regional closure of Zamora commercial hub. 3.5 hours.

incidents.title: Ecuandureo Highway Vehicle Burning
incidents.detail: Vehicle burned on highway through Ecuandureo; blockade established. 3.5 hours.

incidents.title: Tierra Caliente Drone and Mine Deployment
incidents.detail: CJNG-aligned forces deployed drones with explosive payloads and laid IED mine networks in Tierra Caliente to prevent rival cartels from exploiting chaos. Strategic fortification during military distraction.

shutdowns.title: Jalisco Statewide Shutdown
shutdowns.detail: 81 Oxxo stores damaged, 22 Banco del Bienestar branches affected. Chivas match cancelled. All schools suspended. Churches cancelled masses. 27 security personnel killed (25 National Guard, 1 prison guard, 1 FGE agent). Code Red activated.

shutdowns.title: Guanajuato Multi-Municipality Shutdown
shutdowns.detail: 70+ attacks in 23 of 46 municipalities. 200+ Oxxo stores damaged nationally. 18 Banco del Bienestar branches. Farmacias Guadalajara and transit buses burned.

shutdowns.title: Michoacán Regional Shutdown
shutdowns.detail: ~20 narcobloqueos across Tierra Caliente and western region. Apatzingán, Buenavista, Aguililla, Tepalcatepec, Zamora, Ecuandureo, Ixtlán, Tangancícuaro, Chilchota, La Piedad, Sahuayo, Pátzcuaro affected. C5 Michoacán activated emergency protocols.

disruptions.title: Lázaro Cárdenas Port Operations Suspended
disruptions.detail: Port operations temporarily suspended due to CJNG retaliation threats. Critical shipping delays for container cargo.

disruptions.title: Manzanillo Port Operations Disrupted
disruptions.detail: Port operations temporarily suspended. Resumed with Navy patrols, drone surveillance, Protection Level I.
disruptions.impact: Port operations resumed with enhanced security

phase2.title: Autlán de Navarro Highway Blockades
phase2.detail: Multiple highway blockades on Federal Highway 80. Burning vehicles.

phase2.title: Lagos de Moreno Highway Vehicle Fire
phase2.detail: Vehicle set on fire on Lagos-Zapotlanejo highway near Jalostotitlán toll booth.

phase2.title: Military Operation Kills El Tuli
phase2.detail: Military operation kills El Tuli (Hugo César Macías Ureña), El Mencho's right-hand man who coordinated retaliation. Paracaidistas attempted arrest, suspect fled and opened fire. Seized 7.2M pesos and $965K USD.

phase2.title: Tonaya Confrontations
phase2.detail: Multiple confrontations with narcobloqueos and vehicles set on fire early Tuesday.

phase2.title: Cihuatlán Blockades and Fires
phase2.detail: Narcobloqueos with burning vehicles on Feb 24, confrontations continue Feb 25.

phase2.title: Tonalá Metro Confrontations
phase2.detail: Confrontations and vehicle fires Feb 23-25 in Tonalá metropolitan area.

phase2.title: Mazatlán Operativo Escudo Security Operations
phase2.detail: "Operativo Escudo" activated. GOES detained two armed men. Continued security operations.
